title: Livingin Boulder vlog 10 - 22 - 2017: Attatching battles of 1800s photos to  google earth

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a late-night vlog update about his ongoing Google Earth project, where he is attaching historical photos of battles, castles, and cathedrals to place markers. He estimates the whole project will take about six months to complete and reflects on the mental framework of splitting his thinking between a 'chaos' worker self and a strategic decision-maker self.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: worth doing even when time-consuming; frames time as his currency and weighs value accordingly
currently non-functional like a shell, but believes it will be fully working in about six months
mentally splits into a 'strategist' and a 'worker' to evaluate and execute big projects

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn't do a video yesterday because I was too busy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have been working on a battle project, superimposing maps on Google Earth and attaching pictures to place markers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The next step involves page 1111, almost page 12, and page 14, which has only four pictures on Flickr.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There are about 1300 photos from the 1800s, and I'll finish working on them day after tomorrow.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The 1900s has 199 pictures, which will take me about a week or two to complete after the 1800s.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The 2000s has about 200 photos to work on after the 1900s.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: After that, I have to work on Castles, which is about 2700, and I don't have place markers for them yet.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to check the GBS Played first, which is another step in the process.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: For the battles, I'm just adding pictures now, which is a big chunk of the project.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Sometimes it's good to have different people working on the same thing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm hungry right now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When I decide to do a big project, it's chaotic with all the photos, and it's time-consuming.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The strategist in me wonders how much time the work will take, since my currency is time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Putting all cathedrals, castles, and battles on Google Earth with links is a significant time investment.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I estimate it will take about six months to complete the project.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: By the beginning of summer or May, my timeline might be functional.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I compare my timeline to the Great Pyramid, which was functional before the flood but is now just a shell.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My timeline is currently like the Death Star before it gets blown up—just a shell.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: In six months, my timeline might be fully functional.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My website doesn't work properly if the banks don't work, because consistency is key to my timeline.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The concept behind my timeline is consistency, where everything looks the same to help familiarize patterns.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If you can see everything in the same frame, you can understand it better and zoom around.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The whole timeline needs to work for the concept to function.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can do a demonstration of finding a remote, but it's hard.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll try to talk tomorrow if I get the chance.
